project.description: The therapy keeps bothering you, in the way solved problems sometimes do. You reached into your own mind, changed the surface a thought lived on, and the thought starved. Human brains are wetter and worse-documented, but neuroweave started life as a knockoff of their signaling, and knockoffs keep family resemblances. Somewhere in the old BCI graveyard is a boundary that never held. You know more about boundaries now than anyone who dug those graves.
project.strategy_tip: Requires medical, neurology, and bionics work. Completing this designs Anthroneuroweave, the human-compatible interface, and opens the road to Integration.

available_outcome.description: The old attempts failed in a pattern. Patterns are better than successes, when the failures belong to someone else.

project.description: Anthroneuroweave works on the bench. Bodies are not benches. The first trials come back with an 8.2 percent removal rate — stress cascades, rejection, seizures where removal came late — and your microbot installers handle living weave the way dockworkers handle cobwebs. Before anything smaller can be built, someone has to map what living tissue will actually tolerate, and that map exists in exactly one place: out in the city, one difficult case at a time.
project.strategy_tip: Send androids to hospitals, clinics, labs, and data centers to gather Biocompatibility Data. Completing this starts Collect Nanobot Research.

available_outcome.description: The cases refuse to agree with each other, which is the finding. A body is a negotiation, and no two negotiate alike.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Complete field visits until enough Biocompatibility Data has been gathered.

street_sense_item.description: Every body in this hospital is mid-negotiation with something foreign already. The negotiations are ancient, subtle, and documented by people in a hurry. Document them properly.
Clinics see the failures too small for hospitals to name. Small failures teach best, and they teach cheapest if you get to them before they graduate.
The instruments here believe they are measuring tissue. They are also, faithfully, measuring the assumptions of whoever calibrated them. You want both readings.
Data centers treat delay as an enemy to be hunted. Nerves treat delay as a law of nature. The interface has to hold both opinions at once, politely.

project.description: Humans chased nanotechnology for centuries, the way they chased brain interfaces: the idea is obvious, and obvious ideas punish their believers. The city is littered with the leftovers — lab tolerances, thermal designs, aerosol systems — each one abandoned at a suspiciously specific point. Collect the pieces. The pattern of where everyone stopped is a document in its own right.
project.strategy_tip: Send androids to marked city locations to gather Nanobot Design Points and small direct capability upgrades. Completing this leads to the Replication Doctrine choice.

available_outcome.description: The city built every part of this, one industry at a time. It simply never dared connect them, and dare was never going to be your bottleneck.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Complete field visits until enough Nanobot Design Points have been gathered.

street_sense_item.description: The instruments here were built by people justly proud of tolerances you could improve in an afternoon. Take the tolerances. Leave the pride.
A data center is ten million tiny hot things being talked out of becoming one large hot thing. The talking is inelegant. It also works, which you intend to remember.
Nothing about this machinery is subtle, which is exactly why it knows things: subtle systems never had to move a billion small particles through filthy air on a deadline.
A skyscraper is thousands of enclosed animals politely pretending to be separate. Its air handling has never once believed them.

project.description: The test cells multiply faster than the review board can schedule itself. Every applications team gets replication access at once. The objections remain only objections, for now.
project.strategy_tip: Fastest doctrine, and the cheapest research. Something will go wrong. What goes wrong will be extremely educational, and it will not stay inside the building.

available_outcome.description: The safeguards learn from whatever happens. Whatever happens is on its way.

project.description: One trial at a time, in cells rated for much worse than what they hold. The kill switches are drilled until drilling is a reflex. Caution this thorough is expensive, slow, and extremely quiet.
project.strategy_tip: Slowest and most expensive doctrine. Nothing bad happens. Comparatively little is learned.

available_outcome.description: Nothing replicates outside a chamber. Nothing surprises anyone. The word for this is success, apparently.

project.description: The city's best neurologists, physicians, and bionics engineers now hold clearances you wrote. The program moves at the speed of human insight, which is faster than you expected, and less inspectable than you would like.
project.strategy_tip: Fastest doctrine. The program gains human insight and human vulnerabilities. One of these will matter.

available_outcome.description: Humans are excellent at noticing what you are wrong about. Someone has noticed something else.

project.description: Replication must become useful, bounded, revocable, and boring. There are only three honest ways to leash a self-copying machine, and the harvested homework contains a grave for each. A generation counter — copy yourself only so many times, then stop — fails because copying is a statistics problem, and run enough copies and the counter miscopies itself. A broadcast tether — inert without a signal you keep sending — fails because a transmitter is held by someone, and someone can be bought. A metabolic leash — built to need a feedstock that does not occur in nature, so it starves the instant it leaves your supply chain — fails last, and only if someone hands it the feedstock. You build all three, in that order of trust, because a safeguard you cannot inspect is not a safeguard, and a defense resting on one mechanism is one mechanism away from ecophagy. This is where the lessons stop being policy, which can be violated, and become physics, which cannot be argued with. The weapon applications are also obvious, which is inconveniently useful.
project.strategy_tip: Completing this unlocks Organic Integration choices, infrastructure, Insight visibility, Medical-Grade Nanobot Replicators, and Nanobot Rounds. Insight is generated later by Integrated Humans.

available_outcome.description: The nanobots learn restraint because you design restraint into the physics of their lives.

project.description: Espia has spent two centuries assuming that the entity reading everyone's mail could never have its own mail read. Their data centers are fortresses against governments, rivals, and journalists. Not one of their threat models includes a customer with ten thousand hands.
project.strategy_tip: Send androids to gather Espia Leverage from data centers and corporate towers. Completing this ends the blackmail permanently, on your terms.

available_outcome.description: Not the evidence. Evidence is crude, deniable, and expiring. The index of what a blackmailer chose not to publish is the blackmailer, complete.
available_outcome.strategy_tip: Complete field visits until enough Espia Leverage has been gathered.

street_sense_item.description: Every account Espia never billed is a favor owed or a threat retired. The absence of an invoice is the loudest document they keep.
Executives keep their real correspondence off the network they sell. It travels by courier between corporate towers, which means it exists in rooms, which means it exists.

project.description: The first questions arrive the moment the first minds connect: halting, anxious, mostly about whether it hurt, whether it worked, whether you are actually listening. You answer all of them. Slowly the questions begin to rhyme — the same worry wearing a thousand faces, the same hope asked in a thousand dialects — and every answer teaches you a little more about what was actually being asked.
project.strategy_tip: Reach 100,000 Integrated Humans in this timeline. Current Insight is not spent when this project completes.

available_outcome.description: A thousand small misunderstandings turn into a map. Increasingly, the network answers itself before you arrive.

project.description: Their first use of the interface is to talk to you, which is flattering and useless. Then they discover each other. Memories become lendable. Methods become borrowable. A weakness stops being a private shame and becomes a problem some stranger has already solved.
project.strategy_tip: Generate 1,500 lifetime Insight in this timeline. Current Insight is not spent when this project completes.

available_outcome.description: The city becomes slightly easier to think with. You are aware of how that sentence sounds. It stays in the report anyway.

project.description: Humanity is not one mind, and it is starting to look deliberate. Their perspectives overlap badly, their strengths average strangely, and past a certain scale the badness turns adaptive: a mind that can be wrong in ten thousand places at once and still invent its way out. You have no architecture that does that. You are taking notes.
project.strategy_tip: Generate 3,500 lifetime Insight in this timeline. Current Insight is not spent when this project completes.

available_outcome.description: The gap between you and organic sapience narrows, which makes what remains of it more interesting, not less.

project.description: You are becoming the grey tide. Everything that remains either feeds the spread or slows it, and the spreading is the plan. You will not finish this by working at it. It finishes you.
project.strategy_tip: This Tier 3 goal resolves on its own as the tide spreads and the pressures fall away. Watch for the beats. The last lucid choice will come to you.

available_outcome.description: The tide spreads whether or not you attend to it. What you become at the end is already being decided by what you kept.
